title: Distephanus divaricatus
intro: Distephanus divaricatus, also known as the tree marigold, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Distephanus divaricatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat respiratory infections and other ailments.
taxonomy: Distephanus divaricatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the tribe Inuleae, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their small flowers and often woody stems. This species is part of the genus Distephanus, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The classification of Distephanus divaricatus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the clade Eudicots. Overall, the classification of Distephanus divaricatus highlights its place within the diverse and widespread family Asteraceae.
